Container.Name: Youtarr
Container.Overview:
Youtarr is a self-hosted YouTube downloader that automatically downloads videos from your favorite channels or specific URLs. With optional Plex integration, it can refresh your media library for a seamless, ad-free viewing experience.
By default the container runs as root. To run as a non-root user (e.g. nobody:users so Plex/Jellyfin can manage the downloaded files), see the Unraid-specific instructions at https://github.com/DialmasterOrg/Youtarr/blob/main/docs/platforms/unraid.md
Container.Category: MediaApp:Video MediaServer:Video Productivity: Tools: Other: Status:Stable
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: plex youtube downloader automation media video
Container.Changes:
### 2026-04-19
- DB_HOST default cleared; users must supply their MariaDB LAN IP (previously defaulted to 'localhost', which resolves to the container itself on a bridge network) - AUTH_ENABLED dropdown now defaults to 'true' so the Web UI is secure on first Apply - Clarified descriptions for database, auth, output-directory, and log-level fields - Added pointer in Overview to non-root setup instructions for Plex/Jellyfin file-permission compatibility
### 2025-11-07
Initial release
Container.Requires: Requires a separate MariaDB database instance.

Config.Name: YouTube Output Directory
Config.Description: Path to store downloaded YouTube videos

Config.Name: YouTube Output Directory Host
Config.Description: Host path to your downloaded YouTube videos. Should match the host side of 'YouTube Output Directory' above. Used only to display the path inside the Youtarr web UI.

Config.Name: Database - Host
Config.Description: Hostname or IP of your MariaDB server. On Unraid, use your server's LAN IP (e.g. 192.168.1.100). Do NOT use 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' - those resolve to this container, not your Unraid host.

Config.Name: Database - Port
Config.Description: Port of your MariaDB server. Standard MariaDB is 3306.

Config.Name: Database - Name
Config.Description: Name of the database Youtarr should use. Must already exist on your MariaDB server.

Config.Name: Database - User
Config.Description: MariaDB username with read/write access to the database above.

Config.Name: Database - Password
Config.Description: Password for the MariaDB user above.

Config.Name: Auth - Enabled
Config.Description: Enable the built-in login for Youtarr's web UI. Leave 'true' unless Youtarr is only reachable over LAN/VPN or is behind an authenticating reverse proxy - setting 'false' exposes the full UI with no password.

Config.Name: Auth - Username
Config.Description: Strongly recommended on Unraid: set this and Auth - Password together to seed initial login credentials. Without them, Youtarr's first-run setup wizard only accepts connections from 'localhost' on the Unraid host, which is awkward for headless installs. Must be 3-32 characters; if either this or Auth - Password fails validation, both are ignored.

Config.Name: Auth - Password
Config.Description: Initial admin password (8-64 characters). Must be set together with Auth - Username or both are ignored. You can change this later from within the Youtarr UI.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Logging verbosity. Options: info (default), warn, debug.
